mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates below 0.25% reduce discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 8.0% signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when UNRATE crosses the 8.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term marginal predictive power is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.0% quarter-over-quarter reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization that supports corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >65% of observations during easing cycles when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.0%, or the signal reverses direction in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment gains exceeding 150,000 signal strengthening labor demand that boosts aggregate income expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with PAYEMS gains > 150k, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month cumulative CPI inflation increase exceeding 0.8% triggers expectations of central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production growth outpaces real GDP growth indicates supply-side efficiency gains that expand profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence metric yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise (p > 0.15) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during a high-unemployment regime confirms weak consumer demand that forces corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of observations when RSAFS declines during high-unemployment periods, or the regime-conditional signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.
